reason: Response A has a more structured, paragraph-by-paragraph approach that methodically moves through senses (sight, sound, smell, time, emotion), which aligns with Sonnet's tendency toward clear organization. Response B uses denser, more elaborate prose with richer compound imagery ("bruised silver," "slick near-black that doubles every streetlamp") and a more sustained poetic register throughout, suggesting the more expansive stylistic range of Opus. Both are high quality, but A feels more measured and balanced while B leans into more literary flourish.
